proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all 122 features in ds001_nsclc, at least one feature is associated with objective_response. Specifically, ordinal feature_051 (levels 0/1/2) is negatively associated with objective_response (higher feature_051 -> lower probability of response).

analyses.code: for c in features: sm.Logit(objective_response, [1, c]).fit() ; sort by p-value
analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic of feature_051 on objective_response: beta=-0.375 per unit (OR=0.687), p=1.7e-93. Response rates by level: 21.1% (0), 15.7% (1), 11.1% (2). Strong monotonic negative gradient across the three levels.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Binary feature_013 (prevalence 65%) is negatively associated with objective_response: patients with feature_013=1 have a lower response probability than patients with feature_013=0.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 19.6% if feature_013=0 (n=17635) vs 15.4% if feature_013=1 (n=32365). Logistic coef=-0.295, OR=0.744, p=1.3e-33. Direction: negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous feature_011 (range 0-28, mean 3.8) is negatively associated with objective_response, with a roughly monotonic dose-response across quintiles.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic: beta=-0.0385 per unit (OR=0.962), p=1.8e-32. Quintile response rates show monotonic decline: Q1 18.9% -> Q2 17.0% -> Q3 15.8% -> Q4 13.7%. Direction: negative; dose-response confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Binary feature_067 (prevalence 25%) is negatively associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic: beta=-0.252, OR=0.777, p=2.0e-18. Response rate 14.4% with feature_067=1 vs 17.7% with feature_067=0. Direction: negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Binary feature_006 (prevalence ~28%) is positively associated with objective_response: patients with feature_006=1 have higher response than those with feature_006=0.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 18.2% if feature_006=1 vs 16.4% if feature_006=0. Univariate beta=+0.127, OR=1.136, p=9.8e-7. Direction: positive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous feature_092 (range 0-0.8, mean 0.31) is positively associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic beta=+0.166 per unit (OR=1.18 per 1-unit increase), p=3.2e-3. Quartile rates: 16.7% / 16.5% / 16.5% / 17.8% — main signal lies in the top quartile (non-linear, threshold-like effect rather than linear).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutual adjustment in a single multivariable logistic regression, feature_051, feature_013, feature_011, feature_067 remain significant negative predictors and feature_006, feature_099, feature_092 remain significant positive predictors of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: All seven variables retain independent effects after adjustment. feature_051 beta=-0.381 p=2e-95; feature_013 beta=-0.302 p=2e-34; feature_011 beta=-0.039 p=2e-33; feature_067 beta=-0.259 p=4e-19; feature_006 beta=+0.130 p=2e-6; feature_099 beta=+0.093 p=1e-4; feature_092 beta=+0.174 p=2e-3. Pseudo-R^2=0.020; LLR p<1e-180.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race (feature_123, levels asian/black/hispanic/other/white) is associated with objective_response, with at least one race-group response rate differing from the others after adjustment for clinical features.

analyses.result_summary: Crude chi-square across 5 race groups: p=0.045 (asian 17.7%, black 17.8%, hispanic 17.2%, other 18.5%, white 16.5%). After adjustment for feature_051/013/011/067/006/092 in multivariable logistic, no race-level coefficient is significant (largest |z|=1.51 for white, p=0.13). Hypothesis refuted: no racial disparity in objective_response after adjusting for clinical features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Histology (feature_043, adenocarcinoma vs squamous) is associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Crude rates: adenocarcinoma 16.7%, squamous 17.4%. Chi-square p=0.078; multivariable-adjusted beta(squamous)=+0.040, p=0.156. Hypothesis refuted: histology not independently associated with response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Smoking status (feature_057, current/former/never) is associated with objective_response, with never-smokers having different response than current smokers.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficients: former vs current beta=+0.013 p=0.65; never vs current beta=+0.014 p=0.73. Hypothesis refuted: smoking status not associated with objective_response after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Age at diagnosis (feature_078, range 30-90) is negatively associated with objective_response: older patients respond at lower rates.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted beta=+0.0003 per year, p=0.78. Hypothesis refuted: no independent association of age with response in this cohort after adjusting for clinical features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Insurance status (feature_005, medicaid/medicare/private/uninsured) is associated with objective_response, with private insurance associated with higher response than medicaid (the reference).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficients vs medicaid: private beta=+0.088 p=0.020; medicare beta=+0.062 p=0.099; uninsured beta=+0.080 p=0.244. Private-vs-medicaid is borderline significant. Direction: private positive vs medicaid. Magnitude small (OR ~1.09).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Ordinal feature_064 (range 0-10, plausible comorbidity-burden index) is negatively associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(objective_response ~ feature_064).fit() ; mean response by level
analyses.result_summary: Univariate beta=+0.0013 per unit, p=0.776. Response rates flat across levels 0-10 (range 16.0%-18.1%, no monotonic trend). Hypothesis refuted: feature_064 has no association with response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_006 on objective_response is amplified when feature_092 is high: there is a significant positive feature_006 x feature_092 interaction in a logistic regression adjusted for the main clinical predictors.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term feature_006:feature_092 beta=+0.592, OR=1.81, p=1.6e-6. Stratified rates by feature_006 x feature_092 quintile show response 16.5-17.8% across quintiles when feature_006=0 but rises to 23.2% in the top feature_092 quintile when feature_006=1, vs ~17% in lower feature_092 strata. Hypothesis supported: positive interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Feature_092 also positively modifies the response effect of feature_007 (a binary feature with ~45% prevalence) — interaction beta>0.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_007:feature_092 beta=+0.614, OR=1.85, p=7.3e-8 in adjusted model. Direction matches hypothesis: feature_092 amplifies feature_007 positive effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Feature_092 likewise positively modifies the response effect of feature_039 (binary, ~50% prevalence) — interaction beta>0.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_039:feature_092 beta=+0.595, OR=1.81, p=1.7e-7. Hypothesis supported. Pattern across feature_006/007/039 suggests feature_092 acts as a continuous biomarker that broadly amplifies positive predictors of response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among binary features that look like distinct treatment markers (feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, feature_013), there is significant pairwise overlap (i.e., they are not independent).

analyses.result_summary: Pairwise chi-square p-values: 006x007 p=0.31; 006x039 p=0.70; 007x039 p=0.95; 006x013 p=0.89; 007x013 p=0.19; 039x013 p=0.70. All non-significant. Hypothesis refuted: these binary features are independently distributed in this cohort. They likely capture different orthogonal exposures.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_006 on objective_response is moderated by feature_051 (ECOG-like ordinal): feature_006 produces a smaller absolute response gain in higher feature_051 strata.

analyses.result_summary: Stratified response rates: feature_051=0: 20.6% (006=0) vs 22.2% (006=1); feature_051=1: 15.1% vs 17.2%; feature_051=2: 10.6% vs 12.2%. Interaction beta=+0.040, p=0.318 (not significant). Absolute differences are similar (~1.5-2.1 percentage points) across strata. Hypothesis refuted: relative effect of feature_006 is uniform across feature_051 strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_006 on objective_response differs by histology (feature_043 adenocarcinoma vs squamous), with a larger effect in one histology.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term squamous:feature_006 beta=-0.021, p=0.71. Hypothesis refuted: feature_006 effect does not vary by histology.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Receipt of the positive predictor feature_006 differs by race (feature_123): some race groups have lower probability of feature_006=1 than others, indicating disparate access.

analyses.result_summary: Proportion with feature_006=1 by race: asian 28.8%, black 28.2%, hispanic 28.3%, other 30.4%, white 28.5%. Chi-square p=0.669. Hypothesis refuted: no racial disparity in feature_006 receipt.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Receipt of feature_006 differs by insurance type (feature_005), with privately insured patients more likely to receive feature_006=1.

analyses.result_summary: Proportion feature_006=1 by insurance: medicaid 28.9%, medicare 28.8%, private 28.2%, uninsured 27.4%. Chi-square p=0.256. Hypothesis refuted: no insurance-related disparity in feature_006 receipt.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous predictor feature_092 has a non-linear (threshold-like) relationship with objective_response: the response gain is concentrated in the upper quartile rather than smoothly increasing across all quartiles.

analyses.result_summary: Quartile response rates: Q1 16.74%, Q2 16.52%, Q3 16.54%, Q4 17.78%. Q4-vs-Q1 difference ~1.04 percentage points (the only meaningful step). Quintile-stratified: top quintile in feature_006=1 reaches 23.2%. Hypothesis supported: most of feature_092's signal is concentrated in the upper tail (threshold-like).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients in the joint upper stratum of feature_006=1 AND feature_092 top quintile have substantially higher objective_response than the baseline cohort.

analyses.result_summary: Joint upper stratum (feature_006=1 AND feature_092 top quintile, n=2927): 23.16% response vs cohort baseline 16.89%. Two-proportion z-test vs baseline: difference +6.27 pp, p<1e-15. Hypothesis supported: this combined biomarker/feature stratum identifies a clearly enriched-responder subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous feature_063 (right-skewed, median ~3.7, max ~297) is independently negatively associated with objective_response in adjusted analysis.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted beta=-0.0050 per unit, OR=0.995 per unit, p=5.1e-4. Hypothesis supported: feature_063 retains an independent negative association with response after multivariable adjustment, with a small per-unit effect over a wide dynamic range.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A parsimonious final model that includes feature_051, feature_013, feature_011, feature_067 (negative), feature_006, feature_092, feature_007, feature_039, feature_099 (positive), the feature_006:feature_092 interaction, and feature_063, captures the main signals in this dataset; the model's overall LLR p-value vs null is highly significant.

analyses.result_summary: Full final model converged (n=50000). Pseudo-R^2 ~0.021; LLR p<1e-180 vs null. All listed main effects retain |z|>3 except feature_092 main term which becomes weaker once interaction is included (signal moves into the interaction term, beta=+0.592, p=1.6e-6). Final model identifies one strong negative axis (feature_051, feature_013, feature_011, feature_067, feature_063) and one positive axis (feature_006, feature_092, feature_007, feature_039, feature_099) with a feature_006 x feature_092 synergy.
